To determine the correct chain, calculate the required power transmission capacity and chain strength:

1. **Power Calculation**: The motor delivers \( \frac{7 \text{ hp} \times 550 \text{ ft-lb/s}}{550} = 7 \text{ ft-kW} \), but more importantly, the **torque** on the driving sprocket is \( \frac{7 \times 5252}{500} \approx 73.5 \text{ lb-ft} \).
2. **Chain Strength**: A **triple-strand no. 50 chain** (Option B) is typically rated for ~10–15 hp at 500 rpm with a 30-inch center distance, while a **no. 60 chain** (Option C) is overkill for this application.
